content.full_text:
82030

January 9, 1978

Mr. Wesley K. Walton, Superintendent
Los Gatos Union School District
16555 Shannon Road, P.O. Box 367
Los Gatos, California 95030

Dear Mr. Walton:

In your letter of December 16, 1977, you ask whether district
contributions to group benefit plans for school district trustees
constitute "salary" for the purpose of determining if the trustees
must file semi-annual campaign statements under Government Code
Section 84206.

It is our advice that fringe benefits such as the employer con-
tributions to group benefit plans in this case do not constitute
"salary" for the purposes of Section 84206.

Sincerely yours,

John D. Giansello
Political Analyst
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

LOS GATOS UNION SCHOOL DISTRICT
LOS GATOS, CALIFORNIA
P.O. BOX 367
95030
SUPERINTENDENT'S OFFICE - PHONE 356-2129

December 16, 1977

Mr. John Giansello
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA. 95814

Dear Mr. Giansello:

The County Registrar of Voters has sent us the Off-Year Campaign Statement report form regarding the trustees of the Los Gatos Union School District.

The board members have consistently declined to vote themselves a stipend or salary for their attendance at meetings, even though there were twenty-three public meetings last year and almost an equal number of executive sessions for personnel purposes, and a significant number related to employer-employee negotiations.

The law also allows trustees to become members of the employee group benefit plans. In the case of two board members, the district contributions to the insurance carriers in their behalf are $628 and $642 for the period July 1, 1977 and December 31, 1977.

Government Code 84206 states that, "This section is not applicable to elected officers whose salaries are less than one hundred dollars ($100) a month....."

Would we be correct in considering that a benefit is not a salary, and therefore would not need to be reported on the Off-Year Campaign Statement? In the field of employer-employee relations, benefits and salaries are never used synonymously in negotiations or in other kinds of reports or surveys.

I would appreciate a response, if possible, in time for us to return the Off-Year Campaign Statement no later than the December 31st deadline given us by the County Registrar of Voters.

Sincerely,

Wesley K. Walton
District Superintendent

WKW/wd
